title: Workdays Calculator (skip weekends + holidays)
shortDescription: Count business days between two dates, excluding weekends and any holidays you list. Customizable: include Saturday or Sunday as workdays if needed.
description: Pick a start and end date, optionally include Saturday or Sunday as workdays, and add holiday dates to exclude. Returns workdays, weekend days, and holidays separately.
metaDescription: Free workdays calculator. Count business days between two dates, skip weekends and custom holidays. Useful for project deadlines, contract terms, leave planning.
empty: Pick a start and end date to count workdays.

input.holidaysHint: ISO format: 2026-01-01. Comma, space, or newline-separated.

sections.heading: Why workday counting matters
paragraphs: Project deadlines, contract terms, payroll cycles, and leave planning all use 'business days' rather than calendar days. A 30-day delivery promise made on a Friday is actually 32 calendar days because the weekend doesn't count. Mistaking the two leads to missed deadlines and contract disputes.

Workdays also matter for legal counting: in many jurisdictions, statutory time limits (e.g., '14 business days to respond') exclude weekends and public holidays. Always check whether 'days' means calendar or business in any agreement.

sections.heading: Why we let you customize weekends
paragraphs: Most countries use Saturday-Sunday as the weekend, but it varies. Most Middle Eastern and some North African countries observe Friday-Saturday as the weekend. Some industries (retail, hospitality) consider weekends as workdays.
Our toggles let you mark Saturday or Sunday as workdays individually. So you can configure: Mon-Fri only (default), 6-day work week (include Saturday), or even Sunday-only weekend (uncheck Sunday).

sections.heading: How to use the holidays input
paragraphs: Paste a list of dates in YYYY-MM-DD format (ISO 8601). Separators can be commas, spaces, or newlines. Each date you list will be excluded from the workday count if it falls on what would otherwise be a workday.

For Japanese national holidays, similar lists are widely available. We don't include them automatically because (a) holidays vary by country, region, and year, and (b) some businesses observe additional company-specific days off.

howTo.name: Set start and end dates
howTo.text: Both inclusive — both dates count if they're workdays.

howTo.name: Pick weekend days
howTo.text: By default Saturday and Sunday are weekends. Toggle them as workdays if needed.

howTo.name: Add holidays to exclude
howTo.text: List ISO dates separated by commas, spaces, or newlines.

howTo.name: Read the breakdown
howTo.text: Workdays, weekend days, holidays, and total calendar days.

faq.q: Are start and end dates inclusive?
faq.a: Yes. If both are workdays, both are counted. If your contract counts only the days between (exclusive), subtract 2 from the result.

faq.q: Why don't you include holidays automatically?
faq.a: Holidays vary widely by country, region, and year. Auto-including them risks subtracting holidays that don't apply to your jurisdiction. Adding them manually keeps you in control.

faq.q: Can I use this for international contracts?
faq.a: Yes — but make sure both parties agree on which holidays to count. Multinational contracts often specify 'business days as observed in the [city] of [country]' to avoid ambiguity.

faq.q: What if a holiday falls on a weekend?
faq.a: It's not double-counted. If 2026-01-01 is a Thursday holiday, it's counted as a holiday. If 2026-12-25 falls on a Sunday, the algorithm correctly counts it as one day (weekend), not two.

faq.q: How does this work for a 6-day work week?
faq.a: Check 'Saturday is a workday'. The calculator will count Mon-Sat as workdays and only exclude Sundays and listed holidays.

faq.q: What's the maximum date range I can compute?
faq.a: Hundreds of years at minimum. The browser handles date arithmetic up to ±100 million days from 1970.

faq.q: Can I export the workday list?
faq.a: Not as a download, but you can copy the displayed numbers. For a per-day list, a spreadsheet is more flexible.

faq.q: Does the data leave my browser?
faq.a: No. Everything runs locally; nothing is sent to a server.

preset.label: Public-holiday preset (2026–2027)
preset.note: Built-in data covers 2026–2027. Holiday dates vary by year and by region — substitute and observed days are included; add any missing local holidays manually.
